title: Don Quixote
author: Miguel de Cervantes
description: A satirical take on chivalry and the first modern novel.

title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
description: A social critique of 19th-century British gentry and marriage.

title: Wuthering Heights
author: Emily Brontë
description: A dark tale of passion and revenge on the Yorkshire moors.

title: Moby Dick
author: Herman Melville
description: An epic struggle between man and a white whale.

title: Jane Eyre
author: Charlotte Brontë
description: The internal struggle of an orphaned girl seeking independence.

title: Crime and Punishment
author: Fyodor Dostoyevsky
description: A psychological exploration of guilt and redemption.

title: Anna Karenina
author: Leo Tolstoy
description: A sweeping narrative of adultery and social change in Russia.

title: Ulysses
author: James Joyce
description: A modernist retelling of the Odyssey set in one day in Dublin.

title: War and Peace
author: Leo Tolstoy
description: The intersection of private lives and Napoleonic history.

author: George Orwell
description: A dystopian vision of totalitarianism and surveillance.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: A story of racial injustice in the American South.

title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: A critique of the American Dream in the Roaring Twenties.

title: One Hundred Years of Solitude
author: Gabriel García Márquez
description: The multi-generational story of the Buendía family.

title: The Catcher in the Rye
author: J.D. Salinger
description: A teenager's rebellion against the "phoniness" of society.

title: Lolita
author: Vladimir Nabokov
description: A controversial exploration of obsession and aesthetics.
